rationale: This pair is likely substantive because both papers center on multichannel conversion attribution using incremental effects with dynamic cross-channel effects: the citing paper formalizes attribution with carryover/interaction and compares it to fixed-budget optimization, while the cited paper develops an empirical multistage attribution model with carryover/spillover and field validation. Given this close conceptual and methodological overlap (and an exact DOI reference match), the citation is plausibly a core theory/method source rather than a passing mention.
